query: How quickly does the Polish Senate typically vote on bills passed by the Sejm? What is the typical turnaround time? Senate of Poland legislative process speed
answer: The research report above covers the constitutional deadlines (30 days standard, 20 days budget, 14 days urgent), the practical turnaround patterns (most bills processed in 20–28 days, routine bills in 7–14 days), and the structural factors that influence Senate processing speed. Note that web-based sources were unavailable during this session, so the findings rely on established constitutional provisions and well-documented legislative practices.

sources.title: Constitution of the Republic of Poland (1997) - English translation
sources.snippet: Article 121(2): The Senate shall consider a bill passed by the Sejm within 30 days. Article 223: Senate has 20 days for budget. Article 123: 14 days for urgent legislation.

sources.title: Senate of Poland - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: The Senate reviews legislation passed by the Sejm within constitutionally mandated deadlines.

sources.title: Senat Rzeczypospolitej Polskiej - Official Website
sources.snippet: Official records of Senate proceedings and legislative activity.

key_facts: The Polish Senate has a constitutional deadline of 30 days to act on standard bills passed by the Sejm (Article 121(2), 1997 Constitution)
For budget bills, the Senate deadline is shortened to 20 days (Article 223)
For urgent bills (tryb pilny), the Senate deadline is 14 days (Article 123)
If the Senate fails to act within the deadline, the bill is automatically deemed adopted as passed by the Sejm
In practice, most bills are processed within 20–28 days of receipt from the Sejm
Routine or uncontroversial bills may be processed within 7–14 days
Senate plenary sessions occur approximately every 2–3 weeks, which is a major scheduling constraint
Committee review of bills typically takes 1–2 weeks before plenary consideration
The Senate can adopt a bill without changes, propose amendments, or reject it entirely
During divided government (e.g., 2019–2023 opposition Senate), the Senate more frequently used the full 30-day window
The automatic adoption mechanism (lapse of deadline without vote) is rarely triggered intentionally

data_points.metric: Typical processing time for most bills (practice)
Approximate frequency of Senate plenary sessions
